heading: Youth prevention and recovery
label: 290bb–7a Youth prevention and recovery

children.heading: Resource center
children.label: b Resource center
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”, except as otherwise provided), in consultation with the Secretary of Education and other heads of agencies, including the Assistant Secretary for Mental Health and Substance Use and the Administrator of the Health Resources and Services Administration, as appropriate, shall establish a resource center to provide technical support to recipients of grants under subsection (c).

children.heading: Youth prevention and recovery initiative
children.label: c Youth prevention and recovery initiative

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Education, shall administer a program to provide support for communities to support the prevention of, treatment of, and recovery from, substance use disorders for children, adolescents, and young adults.

children.label: i
children.text: a local educational agency, or a consortium of local educational agencies, that is seeking to establish or expand substance use prevention or recovery support services at one or more secondary schools;

children.label: ii
children.text: a State educational agency;

children.label: iii
children.text: an institution of higher education (or consortia of such institutions), which may include a recovery program at an institution of higher education;

children.label: iv
children.text: a local board or one-stop operator;

children.label: v
children.text: a nonprofit organization with appropriate expertise in providing services or programs for children, adolescents, or young adults, excluding a school;

children.label: vi
children.text: a State, political subdivision of a State, Indian Tribe, or Tribal organization; or

children.label: vii
children.text: a high school or dormitory serving high school students that receives funding from the Bureau of Indian Education.

children.heading: Foster care
children.label: B Foster care
children.text: The term “foster care” has the meaning given such term in section 1355.20(a) of title 45, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations).

children.heading: High school
children.label: C High school
children.text: The term “high school” has the meaning given such term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: Homeless youth
children.label: D Homeless youth
children.text: The term “homeless youth” has the meaning given the term “homeless children or youths” in .section 11434a of this title

children.heading: Indian Tribe; Tribal organization
children.label: E Indian Tribe; Tribal organization
children.text: The terms “Indian Tribe” and “Tribal organization” have the meanings given such terms in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: F Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given such term in and includes a “postsecondary vocational institution” as defined in section 1002(c) of such title.section 1001 of title 20

children.heading: Local educational agency
children.label: G Local educational agency
children.text: The term “local educational agency” has the meaning given such term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: Local board; one-stop operator
children.label: H Local board; one-stop operator
children.text: The terms “local board” and “one-stop operator” have the meanings given such terms in .section 3102 of title 29

children.heading: Out-of-school youth
children.label: I Out-of-school youth
children.text: The term “out-of-school youth” has the meaning given such term in .section 3164(a)(1)(B) of title 29

children.label: i
children.text: to help children, adolescents, or young adults who are recovering from substance use disorders to initiate, stabilize, and maintain healthy and productive lives in the community; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that includes peer-to-peer support delivered by individuals with lived experience in recovery, and communal activities to build recovery skills and supportive social networks.

children.heading: Secondary school
children.label: K Secondary school
children.text: The term “secondary school” has the meaning given such term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: State educational agency
children.label: L State educational agency
children.text: The term “State educational agency” has the meaning given such term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.label: i
children.text: primary prevention, which may include strategies to increase education and awareness of the potency and dangers of synthetic opioids (including drugs contaminated with fentanyl) and, as appropriate, other emerging drug use or misuse issues;

children.label: ii
children.text: appropriate recovery support services;

children.label: iii
children.text: appropriate use of medication-assisted treatment for such individuals, if applicable, and ways of overcoming barriers to the use of medication-assisted treatment in such population; and

children.label: iv
children.text: efficient and effective communication, which may include the use of social media, to maximize outreach efforts;

children.label: B
children.text: disseminate such best practices to State educational agencies, local educational agencies, schools and dormitories funded by the Bureau of Indian Education, institutions of higher education, recovery programs at institutions of higher education, local boards, one-stop operators, family and youth homeless providers, and nonprofit organizations, as appropriate;

children.label: C
children.text: conduct a rigorous evaluation of each grant funded under this subsection, particularly its impact on the indicators described in paragraph (7)(B); and

children.label: D
children.text: provide technical assistance for grantees under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: prevention of substance misuse by children, adolescents, and young adults, which may include primary prevention and strategies to increase education and awareness of the potency and dangers of synthetic opioids (including drugs contaminated with fentanyl) and, as appropriate, emerging drug use or misuse issues;

children.label: B
children.text: recovery support services for children, adolescents, and young adults, which may include counseling, job training, linkages to community-based services, family support groups, peer-to-peer support, and recovery coaching; or

children.label: C
children.text: treatment or referrals for treatment of substance use disorders, which may include the use of medication-assisted treatment, as appropriate.

children.heading: Special consideration
children.label: 5 Special consideration
children.text: In awarding grants under this subsection, the Secretary shall give special consideration to the unique needs of Tribal, urban, suburban, and rural populations.

children.label: i
children.text: the impact of substance use disorders in the population that will be served by the grant program;

children.label: ii
children.text: how the eligible entity has solicited input from relevant stakeholders, which may include faculty, teachers, staff, families, students, and experts in substance use disorder prevention, treatment, and recovery in developing such application;

children.label: iii
children.text: the goals of the proposed project, including the intended outcomes;

children.label: iv
children.text: how the eligible entity plans to use grant funds for evidence-based activities, in accordance with this subsection to prevent, provide recovery support for, or treat substance use disorders amongst such individuals, or a combination of such activities;

children.label: v
children.text: how the eligible entity will collaborate with relevant partners, which may include State educational agencies, local educational agencies, institutions of higher education, juvenile justice agencies, prevention and recovery support providers, local service providers, including substance use disorder treatment programs, providers of mental health services, youth serving organizations, family and youth homeless providers, child welfare agencies, and primary care providers, in carrying out the grant program; and

children.label: vi
children.text: a plan to sustain the activities carried out under the grant program, after the grant program has ended; and

children.label: B
children.text: an assurance that the eligible entity will participate in the evaluation described in paragraph (3)(C).

children.label: A
children.text: a description of how the eligible entity used grant funds, in accordance with this subsection, including the number of children, adolescents, and young adults reached through programming; and

children.label: i
children.text: indicators of student success, which, if the eligible entity is an educational institution, shall include student well-being and academic achievement;

children.label: ii
children.text: substance use disorders amongst children, adolescents, and young adults, including the number of overdoses and deaths amongst children, adolescents, and young adults served by the grant during the grant period; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other indicators, as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 8 Report to Congress
children.text: The Secretary shall, not later than , submit a report to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives a report summarizing the effectiveness of the grant program under this subsection, based on the information submitted in reports required under paragraph (7).October 1, 2028

children.label: A
children.text: $10,000,000 for fiscal year 2026;

children.label: B
children.text: $12,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;

children.label: C
children.text: $13,000,000 for fiscal year 2028;

children.label: D
children.text: $14,000,000 for fiscal year 2029; and

children.label: E
children.text: $15,000,000 for fiscal year 2030.
